audio_track_actual: Former Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu speaking before the U.S. House Committee on Government Reform on September 12, 2002. C-SPAN broadcast feed.
video_overlay_text_observed: On-screen chyron: "BENJAMIN NETANYAHU — Former Israeli Prime Minister 1996–99." Network ID: C-SPAN. "LIVE" indicator visible.
audio_content_summary: A 24-second excerpt from Netanyahu's sworn testimony to the U.S. House Committee on Government Reform on September 12, 2002, at the hearing titled "Conflict with Iraq: An Israeli Perspective." In the excerpted passage, Netanyahu argues that the September 11, 2001 attacks opened American eyes to a larger conflict, and that the public will of the United States is moving inexorably to settle that conflict — language used to support his case for a U.S. invasion of Iraq.
implied_frame: The video presents Netanyahu's 2002 testimony as evidence that the U.S. invasion of Iraq was sold to Congress in part by foreign-government advocacy on factual premises that did not hold up. The premise on which Netanyahu testified — that Saddam Hussein was on the verge of acquiring nuclear weapons — was not supported by post-invasion findings (no WMD stockpiles were recovered), and the Senate Intelligence Committee's 2004 and 2008 reports later concluded that prewar intelligence assessments were substantially overstated. The video also implies a parallel between the 2002 push for war on Iraq and contemporary advocacy for military action against Iran.
capture_method: Headless browser recording of the in-page <video> element via MediaRecorder over captureStream(), viewport 1280x800.
